6. (d) a + b + c = 14
a³ + b³ + c³ – 3abc = 98
a³ + b³ + c³ – 3abc = (a + b + c) [(a + b + c)² – 3(ab + bc + ac)]
 98 = 14[196 – 3 × (ab + bc + ac)]
7 = [196 – 3(ab + bc + ac)]
– 189 = –3(ab + bc + ac)
ab + bc + ac = 63
7. (a) a + b + c = 12,
a2 + b2 + c2 = 50,
(a + b + c)2
= a2 + b2 + c2 + 2(ab + bc + ca)
144 = 50 + 2(ab + bc + ca)
ab + bc + ca = 47
a3 + b3 + c3 – 3abc
= (a + b + c) [(a2 + b2 + c2 – (ab + bc + ca)]
= 12[50 – 47] = 12 × 3 = 36

5x × x = 20
 5x2 = 20  x2 = 4
 x = 2%

2. (c)
Special Trick:-
abc + cab + bca
abc a100b10c1
cabc100a10b1
bcab100c10a1
abccabbca111a111b111c
= 111 (a + b + c)
= 3 × 37
Result:- Sum of abc, cab, bca is divisible by 3 × 37
and (a + b + c)
Sum of abc, cab, bca is divisible by 3, 37 and (a+b+c
but not 3!

8. (c) sinx + siny = a
cosx + cosy = b
square both sides
sin2x + sin2y + 2sinx siny = a2 .....(i)
cos2x + cos2y + 2cosx cosy = b2 .....(ii)
Adding (i) & (ii)
a2 + b2 = sin2x + cos2x + sin2y + cos2y + 2 [sinx siny +
cosx cosy]
a2 + b2 = 1 + 1 + 2 [sinx siny + cosx cosy]
a2b2 –2

16. (b) If sin= costhen 90°
sin(x + y)cos{3(x + y)}
x + y + 3x + 3y = 90°
4x + 4y = 90°
2(x + y)45°
cot{2(x + y)}= cot 45° = 1

19. (d) Let, the amount of 40%
to be added = x
Total solution = (40 + x)
Amount of ferrous sulphate in the 20% solution
= 20% × 40 = 8 mililitres
Amount of ferrous sulphate in the 40% solution = 40% × x
= 0.40x mililitres
Total amount of ferrous sulphate in the final solution
= 0.25 × (40 + x)

• The abscissa of a point is its perpendicular

axis is negative

Definition - Intercepts
• The x-intercept of a straight line is the x-
coordinate of the point where the graph
crosses the x-axis.
• The y-intercept of a straight line is the y-
co-ordinate of the point where the graph
crosses the y-axis.

MEAN (ekè;)
The arithmetic mean of a given data is the sum
of all observations divided by the number of
observations. For example, a cricketer's scores
in five ODI matches are as follows: 12, 34, 45,
50, 24. To find his average score we calculate
the arithmetic mean of data using the mean
formula:

MODE (cgqyd)
The mode or modal value of a distribution is
that value of the variable for which the
frequency is maximum.
og eku tks fn, x, MsVk esa lcls vf/d ckj fn[kkbZ nsrk gS
;kuh mPpre vko`fÙk oky MsVk cgqyd dgykrk gSA

VARIANCE (izlj.k)
Variance is a measure of variability in statistics.
It assesses the average squared difference between
data values and the mean.

WHAT IS MEAN DEVIATION ?
d
(ekè; fopyu D;k gS\)
A
The mean deviation of a given standard
distribution is the average of the deviation
from the centreal tendency. Central
Tendency can be computed using the
Arithmetic Mean, Median, or Mode of the
data. It is used to show how far the
observations are situated from the central
point of the data (the central point can be
either mean, median or mode).
fdlh fn, x, ekud forj.k dk vkSlr fopyu dsaæh;
ço`fÙk ls fopyu dk vkSlr gSA dsaæh; ço`fÙk dh x.kuk
MsVk ds vadxf.krh; ekè;] ekfè;dk ;k eksM dk mi;ksx
djds dh tk ldrh gSA bldk mi;ksx ;g fn•kus ds fy,
fd;k tkrk gS fd voyksdu MsVk d s dsaæh; fcanq ls fdruh
nwj fLFkr gSa (dsaæh; fcanq ;k rks ekè;] ekfè;dk ;k eksM
gks ldrk gS)A

15. (b) Let, width = x
 Length M= x + 8
Perimeter of rectangle = 2 (length + width)
Half perimeter = (length + width)
= x + (x + 8)  2x + 8
 2x + 8 = 42
 2x = 42 – 8 = 34 x = 17
Width (x) = 17, Length = (x + 8)
= 17 + 8 = 25
Area of rectangle = Length × width
= 25 × 17 = 425 cm2

1. (c) Volume of cube = a3

When 4 solid cubes are placed in each 2 row, then
dimensions of cuboid are

 Total surface area of cuboid
= 2(lb + bh + hl)
= 2(24 × 24 + 24 × 12 + 24 × 12)
= 2(576 + 288 + 288)
= 2 × 1152 = 2304 cm²

= 2 [Rh + rh + R² – r²]
= 2 [(r + R) (h + R – r)]

 121 = l² + b² + h² ......(i)
Surface area of a cuboid = 2 (lb + bh + hl)
 2(lb + bh + hl) = 240 ......(ii)

 (l + b + h)² = 121 + 240
 (l + b + h)² = 361
 l + b + h = 361 = 19cm
Required sum = 19cm.

10. (b)Properties:
Rectangle  Opposite sides equal, all angles 90°.
Rhombus  All sides equal, opposite angles equal.
Square  All sides equal and all angles 90°.
So, a square satisfies:
The definition of a rectangle (all angles 90°, opposite
sides equal),
The definition of a rhombus (all sides equal, opposite
angles equal).
Therefore, a square is a special case of both a rectangle
and a rhombus

27.(b)Side of cube = 5cm
Joining two cubes gives a cuboid
The dimensions of cuboid L = 5 + 5 = 10 cm
B = 5 cm
H = 5 cm
Total surface area of cuboid
= 2(LB + BH + HL)
= 2(10 × 5 + 5 × 5 + 5 × 10)
= 2(50 + 25 + 50)
= 250 cm2

A34. (b) Regular polygon
The sum of all interior angle = (n – 2) × 180
1260 = (n – 2)×180
n  9
B regular polygon

35. (c)
D C
69
A B
Given
A = 3 (x + 2)º, B = 2 (2x – 7)º, C = 69º, D = C – 8º
= 69º – 8º = 61º
A + B + C + D = 360º
3x + 6º + 4x – 14º = 230º
7x = 238º
x = 34º
B = 2(2x – 7)º = 2(2 × 34 – 7)º
= 122º
